meaning: n.科里奥兰纳斯(莎士比亚所著历史悲剧中一人物)
examples: In fact, his capacity for megalomania and anger invites comparisons with Shakespeare's more ferocious protagonists — Coriolanus, say.
After a final performance of a gripping production of “Coriolanus” on March 31st, the RST will close until 2010, and the theatre will be extensively remodelled before it reopens.

Enter Coriolanus (Mr. Page), a paragon of the military virtues who more or less single-handedly defeats the enemy.
Is there room in a democracy for an aristocrat like Coriolanus who refuses to play the popularity game?
